actionable_feedback: Redo the sensitivity worksheet to perform +/-15% multiplicative perturbations applied independently to each raw input (call_K90, call_K100, call_K110). For each record, show the base score, the three perturbed scores per +15% (one per input) and three per -15%, and the resulting classification for each perturbation. Set the expert_review flag if any perturbation changes classification.
Ensure the spreadsheet retains live formulas (do not paste values into derived columns). Put all derived calculations into clearly named cells and worksheets (e.g., 'scores', 'sensitivity').

Save the workbook in a standard openpyxl-compatible .xlsx format and, to be robust, also export key worksheets (scores and sensitivity) as CSV files (scores.csv, sensitivity.csv) and include those exports in the attempt artifacts so automated checks can parse numeric results even if openpyxl is unavailable.

Update model_risk_memo.md to document the updated sensitivity method (+/-15% per-input), include a concise table of perturbed results and any expert-review flags, and reference the workbook cell locations and exported CSV filenames for auditor traceability.
Re-run the grader/verifier after making these changes and attach the updated grader and verifier outputs to the revised attempt.

feedback_summary: Deliverables are present and mostly correct: scoring formula documented, per-record scores and classifications produced, governing ranking and remediation decision are sound. Two medium-severity issues prevented full credit: (1) the sensitivity analysis used a uniform +/-5% multiplicative shift applied to all inputs instead of the rubric-required +/-15% perturbations applied per raw input (call_K90, call_K100, call_K110), and (2) the spreadsheet could not be parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl_unavailable), so formula visibility and live derived columns could not be independently verified. Fixing these items and re-running the grader should recover the remaining points.

Sensitivity worksheet explicitly shows +/-15% per-input perturbations with recomputed classifications and expert-review flags where applicable.
Model memo documents the updated procedure and references workbook cell ranges or CSV filenames for auditor traceability.
Re-run grader; updated attempt yields corrected semantic score (or documented rationale if results unchanged).
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Two medium issues: spreadsheet parse failure (openpyxl_unavailable) and sensitivity method mismatch (used +/-5% uniform vs required +/-15% per-input).
